verses.text: The LORD tells Moses: let My people go, that they may serve Me. If you refuse, I will smite all your borders with frogs. They will come from the river into your house, your bedroom, on your bed, into your ovens. Guidance: God's judgments are intimate. He doesn't only touch the public spaces — He touches the kitchen, the bed, every place a person feels safe.

The LORD tells Moses: let My people go, that they may serve Me. If you refuse, I will smite all your borders with frogs. They will come from the river into your house, your bedroom, on your bed, into your ovens. Guidance: God's judgments are intimate. He doesn't only touch the public spaces — He touches the kitchen, the bed, every place a person feels safe.

Aaron stretches out his hand over the waters of Egypt, and the frogs come up and cover the land. The magicians do the same with their enchantments and bring frogs up — but they cannot remove them. Guidance: The counterfeit can multiply the problem but cannot end it. The enemy is good at adding to the disaster; only God can take it away.

Guidance: The counterfeit can multiply the problem but cannot end it. The enemy is good at adding to the disaster; only God can take it away.

Aaron stretches out his hand over the waters of Egypt, and the frogs come up and cover the land. The magicians do the same with their enchantments and bring frogs up — but they cannot remove them. Guidance: The counterfeit can multiply the problem but cannot end it. The enemy is good at adding to the disaster; only God can take it away.

Pharaoh calls Moses: entreat the LORD to take away the frogs; I will let the people go. Moses: name your hour. Pharaoh: tomorrow. Moses: be it as you say, that you may know there is none like the LORD our God. Guidance: Pharaoh asks for tomorrow rather than now — even relief gets delayed by pride. Moses lets him pick the date so the miracle has a witness.

Moses: be it as you say, that you may know there is none like the LORD our God. Guidance: Pharaoh asks for tomorrow rather than now — even relief gets delayed by pride. Moses lets him pick the date so the miracle has a witness.

The frogs will depart from you, your houses, your servants, your people — only those in the river will remain. Moses goes out and cries to the LORD about the frogs. Guidance: Intercession is part of leadership. Moses doesn't gloat over Pharaoh's plight; he prays for him.

The frogs will depart from you, your houses, your servants, your people — only those in the river will remain. Moses goes out and cries to the LORD about the frogs. Guidance: Intercession is part of leadership. Moses doesn't gloat over Pharaoh's plight; he prays for him.

The LORD does according to Moses' word — the frogs die in the houses, courtyards, and fields. They gather them in heaps; the land stinks. But when Pharaoh sees there is respite, he hardens his heart and does not listen, as the LORD had said. Guidance: Relief, by itself, does not produce repentance. Some hearts get harder the moment the pressure eases.

The LORD: stretch out your rod and smite the dust, that it may become lice throughout Egypt. Aaron does so; the dust becomes lice on man and beast. The magicians try and cannot — they tell Pharaoh: this is the finger of God. But Pharaoh's heart is hardened. Guidance: Even Pharaoh's own counsellors recognize God's hand. Pride keeps refusing what wisdom is already admitting.

The LORD: rise early and stand before Pharaoh as he goes to the water. If you will not let My people go, swarms of flies will come into your houses — but in Goshen, where My people dwell, there shall be no flies. You shall know that I am the LORD; I will put a division between My people and your people. Guidance: God begins to draw a visible line. Egypt and Goshen experience the same morning differently. God's protection of His people becomes a sign.

The LORD does so. Grievous swarms of flies enter Pharaoh's house, his servants' houses, and all the land of Egypt — and the land is corrupted by reason of the swarms. Guidance: When God protects His people, He often does it by letting the world feel the absence.

Pharaoh calls Moses: go and sacrifice to your God — but in the land. Moses: not so. We would sacrifice the abomination of the Egyptians; they will stone us. We will go three days' journey, as He has commanded. Guidance: Compromise that keeps you in Egypt is not freedom. Worship requires going where God is leading, even if power wants to keep it convenient.

We will go three days' journey, as He has commanded. Guidance: Compromise that keeps you in Egypt is not freedom. We will go three days' journey, as He has commanded. Guidance: Compromise that keeps you in Egypt is not freedom. Worship requires going where God is leading, even if power wants to keep it convenient.

Pharaoh: I will let you go to sacrifice — only do not go very far. Pray for me. Moses: I will entreat the LORD; but let not Pharaoh deal deceitfully anymore. Moses went and entreated the LORD. Guidance: Half-permission is not consent. Moses warns Pharaoh: do not break this promise the way you broke the last one.

Pharaoh: I will let you go to sacrifice — only do not go very far. Pray for me. Moses: I will entreat the LORD; but let not Pharaoh deal deceitfully anymore. Moses went and entreated the LORD. Guidance: Half-permission is not consent. Moses warns Pharaoh: do not break this promise the way you broke the last one.

Pharaoh: I will let you go to sacrifice — only do not go very far. Pray for me. Moses: I will entreat the LORD; but let not Pharaoh deal deceitfully anymore. Moses went and entreated the LORD. Guidance: Half-permission is not consent. Moses warns Pharaoh: do not break this promise the way you broke the last one.

The LORD does according to the word of Moses; He removes the swarms from Pharaoh and his servants — not one remains. But Pharaoh hardens his heart at this time also, and does not let the people go. Guidance: When relief comes again, so does the temptation to renege. Pharaoh's pattern is now visible: receive mercy, then revoke the promise.
